req.title: Beneficiary management (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period before it can receive transfers. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for Beneficiary Cooling-Off Period Requirement

```python
"""
Test Suite: Beneficiary Addition with 24-Hour Cooling-Off Period
Requirement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period
             before it can receive transfers.
Negative paths: insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from uuid import uuid4

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / service interfaces assumed to exist in the production code.
# Adjust import paths to match your actual project structure.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from banking.beneficiaries import BeneficiaryService
from banking.transfers import TransferService
from banking.models import (
    Account,
    Beneficiary,
    BeneficiaryStatus,
    Transfer,
    TransferStatus,
)
from banking.exceptions import (
    BeneficiaryAlreadyExistsError,
    BeneficiaryCoolingOffError,
    DuplicateBeneficiaryError,
    InsufficientFundsError,
    TransferLimitExceededError,
    UnauthorisedError,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
COOLING_OFF_HOURS = 24
DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT = 10_000.00
ACCOUNT_BALANCE = 5_000.00

UTC = timezone.utc


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_account_repo():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_beneficiary_repo():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_transfer_repo():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_notification_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def beneficiary_service(
    mock_account_repo, mock_beneficiary_repo, mock_notification_service
):
    return BeneficiaryService(
        account_repo=mock_account_repo,
        beneficiary_repo=mock_beneficiary_repo,
        notification_service=mock_notification_service,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def transfer_service(
    mock_account_repo, mock_beneficiary_repo, mock_transfer_repo
):
    return TransferService(
        account_repo=mock_account_repo,
        beneficiary_repo=mock_beneficiary_repo,
        transfer_repo=mock_transfer_repo,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def authorised_customer():
    return Account(
        account_id=str(uuid4()),
        owner_id="customer-001",
        balance=ACCOUNT_BALANCE,
        daily_transfer_limit=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT,
        daily_transferred=0.00,
        is_active=True,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def inactive_customer():
    return Account(
        account_id=str(uuid4()),
        owner_id="customer-locked",
        balance=ACCOUNT_BALANCE,
        daily_transfer_limit=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT,
        daily_transferred=0.00,
        is_active=False,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def new_beneficiary_payload():
    """Minimal valid payload for adding a beneficiary."""
    return {
        "account_number": "GB29NWBK60161331926819",
        "sort_code": "60-16-13",
        "name": "Jane Doe",
        "reference": "Rent",
    }


@pytest.fixture()
def cooling_off_beneficiary(authorised_customer):
    """A beneficiary added less than 24 hours ago (still in cooling-off)."""
    return Beneficiary(
        beneficiary_id=str(uuid4()),
        owner_account_id=authorised_customer.account_id,
        account_number="GB29NWBK60161331926819",
        sort_code="60-16-13",
        name="Jane Doe",
        status=BeneficiaryStatus.COOLING_OFF,
        created_at=datetime.now(UTC) - timedelta(hours=12),  # 12 h ago
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def active_beneficiary(authorised_customer):
    """A beneficiary whose cooling-off period has elapsed."""
    return Beneficiary(
        beneficiary_id=str(uuid4()),
        owner_account_id=authorised_customer.account_id,
        account_number="GB29NWBK60161331926820",
        sort_code="60-16-14",
        name="John Smith",
        status=BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE,
        created_at=datetime.now(UTC) - timedelta(hours=25),  # 25 h ago
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. ADDING A BENEFICIARY — Happy paths
# ===========================================================================


class TestAddBeneficiary:
    def test_add_beneficiary_returns_cooling_off_status(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        new_beneficiary_payload,
    ):
        """Adding a new beneficiary must return COOLING_OFF status."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.find_existing.return_value = None
        mock_beneficiary_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b  # echo back

        result = beneficiary_service.add_beneficiary(
            owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
            **new_beneficiary_payload,
        )

        assert result.status == BeneficiaryStatus.COOLING_OFF

    def test_add_beneficiary_cooling_off_duration_is_24_hours(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        new_beneficiary_payload,
    ):
        """The cooling-off end time must be exactly 24 hours after creation."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.find_existing.return_value = None
        mock_beneficiary_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        before_call = datetime.now(UTC)
        result = beneficiary_service.add_beneficiary(
            owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
            **new_beneficiary_payload,
        )
        after_call = datetime.now(UTC)

        expected_min = before_call + timedelta(hours=COOLING_OFF_HOURS)
        expected_max = after_call + timedelta(hours=COOLING_OFF_HOURS)

        assert expected_min <= result.cooling_off_ends_at <= expected_max

    def test_add_beneficiary_persisted_to_repository(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        new_beneficiary_payload,
    ):
        """Service must call the repository save exactly once."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.find_existing.return_value = None
        mock_beneficiary_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        beneficiary_service.add_beneficiary(
            owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
            **new_beneficiary_payload,
        )

        mock_beneficiary_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_add_beneficiary_notification_sent_to_customer(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        mock_notification_service,
        authorised_customer,
        new_beneficiary_payload,
    ):
        """Customer must receive a notification when a beneficiary is added."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.find_existing.return_value = None
        mock_beneficiary_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        beneficiary_service.add_beneficiary(
            owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
            **new_beneficiary_payload,
        )

        mock_notification_service.send_beneficiary_added.assert_called_once_with(
            account=authorised_customer,
            beneficiary_name=new_beneficiary_payload["name"],
        )

    def test_add_beneficiary_assigns_unique_id(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        new_beneficiary_payload,
    ):
        """Each new beneficiary must receive a unique identifier."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.find_existing.return_value = None
        mock_beneficiary_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        result1 = beneficiary_service.add_beneficiary(
            owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
            **new_beneficiary_payload,
        )
        result2 = beneficiary_service.add_beneficiary(
            owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
            **new_beneficiary_payload,
        )

        assert result1.beneficiary_id != result2.beneficiary_id


# ===========================================================================
# 2. COOLING-OFF PERIOD — State transitions
# ===========================================================================


class TestCoolingOffPeriod:
    def test_beneficiary_is_inactive_during_cooling_off(
        self, cooling_off_beneficiary
    ):
        """A beneficiary created less than 24 h ago must not be ACTIVE."""
        assert cooling_off_beneficiary.status != BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE

    def test_beneficiary_is_active_after_cooling_off_expires(
        self, active_beneficiary
    ):
        """A beneficiary created more than 24 h ago must be ACTIVE."""
        assert active_beneficiary.status == BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE

    def test_cooling_off_ends_at_is_24h_after_creation(
        self, cooling_off_beneficiary
    ):
        """cooling_off_ends_at attribute must equal created_at + 24 h."""
        expected = cooling_off_beneficiary.created_at + timedelta(hours=24)
        assert cooling_off_beneficiary.cooling_off_ends_at == expected

    @patch("banking.beneficiaries.datetime")
    def test_beneficiary_transitions_to_active_exactly_at_24h(
        self,
        mock_dt,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        cooling_off_beneficiary,
    ):
        """Status job must mark beneficiary ACTIVE at the 24-hour boundary."""
        # Simulate system clock at exactly 24 h after creation
        mock_dt.now.return_value = (
            cooling_off_beneficiary.created_at + timedelta(hours=24)
        )
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_expired_cooling_off.return_value = [
            cooling_off_beneficiary
        ]
        mock_beneficiary_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        beneficiary_service.promote_expired_cooling_off_beneficiaries()

        mock_beneficiary_repo.save.assert_called_once()
        saved: Beneficiary = mock_beneficiary_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved.status == BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE

    def test_beneficiary_remains_cooling_off_before_24h_boundary(
        self, cooling_off_beneficiary
    ):
        """At 23 h 59 m the beneficiary must still be in COOLING_OFF."""
        # created_at = 12 h ago → 12 h remaining; well within cooling-off
        remaining = (
            cooling_off_beneficiary.cooling_off_ends_at - datetime.now(UTC)
        ).total_seconds()
        assert remaining > 0, "Beneficiary should still be within cooling-off"

    def test_cancel_beneficiary_during_cooling_off(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        cooling_off_beneficiary,
    ):
        """Customer must be able to cancel a beneficiary during cooling-off."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = cooling_off_beneficiary
        mock_beneficiary_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        result = beneficiary_service.cancel_beneficiary(
            owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
            beneficiary_id=cooling_off_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
        )

        assert result.status == BeneficiaryStatus.CANCELLED


# ===========================================================================
# 3. TRANSFERS TO COOLING-OFF BENEFICIARY — Blocked paths
# ===========================================================================


class TestTransferBlockedDuringCoolingOff:
    def test_transfer_to_cooling_off_beneficiary_raises_error(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        cooling_off_beneficiary,
    ):
        """Transfer to a cooling-off beneficiary must raise BeneficiaryCoolingOffError."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = cooling_off_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(BeneficiaryCoolingOffError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=cooling_off_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=100.00,
                reference="Rent",
            )

    def test_transfer_to_cooling_off_not_persisted(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        mock_transfer_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        cooling_off_beneficiary,
    ):
        """No transfer record must be created when blocked by cooling-off."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = cooling_off_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(BeneficiaryCoolingOffError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=cooling_off_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=100.00,
                reference="Rent",
            )

        mock_transfer_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_transfer_to_cooling_off_error_contains_remaining_time(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        cooling_off_beneficiary,
    ):
        """BeneficiaryCoolingOffError must expose how much time remains."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = cooling_off_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(BeneficiaryCoolingOffError) as exc_info:
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=cooling_off_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=100.00,
                reference="Rent",
            )

        assert exc_info.value.remaining_seconds > 0

    def test_transfer_allowed_to_active_beneficiary(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        mock_transfer_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """Transfer to a fully active beneficiary must succeed."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary
        mock_transfer_repo.save.side_effect = lambda t: t

        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
            owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
            beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
            amount=100.00,
            reference="Rent",
        )

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.PENDING


# ===========================================================================
# 4. NEGATIVE PATH — Unauthorised
# ===========================================================================


class TestUnauthorisedPaths:
    def test_add_beneficiary_for_inactive_account_raises_unauthorised(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        inactive_customer,
        new_beneficiary_payload,
    ):
        """Adding a beneficiary on an inactive/locked account must raise UnauthorisedError."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = inactive_customer

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedError):
            beneficiary_service.add_beneficiary(
                owner_id=inactive_customer.owner_id,
                **new_beneficiary_payload,
            )

    def test_add_beneficiary_unknown_customer_raises_unauthorised(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        new_beneficiary_payload,
    ):
        """Adding a beneficiary for a non-existent owner must raise UnauthorisedError."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedError):
            beneficiary_service.add_beneficiary(
                owner_id="ghost-999",
                **new_beneficiary_payload,
            )

    def test_transfer_initiated_by_wrong_owner_raises_unauthorised(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """A transfer where the caller does not own the source account must be blocked."""
        intruder = Account(
            account_id=str(uuid4()),
            owner_id="intruder-999",
            balance=99_999.00,
            daily_transfer_limit=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT,
            daily_transferred=0.00,
            is_active=True,
        )
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = intruder
        # The beneficiary belongs to authorised_customer, not intruder
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=intruder.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=100.00,
                reference="Illegitimate",
            )

    def test_cancel_beneficiary_by_non_owner_raises_unauthorised(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        cooling_off_beneficiary,
    ):
        """Cancelling another customer's beneficiary must raise UnauthorisedError."""
        intruder = Account(
            account_id=str(uuid4()),
            owner_id="intruder-999",
            balance=0,
            daily_transfer_limit=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT,
            daily_transferred=0.00,
            is_active=True,
        )
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = intruder
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = cooling_off_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedError):
            beneficiary_service.cancel_beneficiary(
                owner_id=intruder.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=cooling_off_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
            )

    def test_add_beneficiary_without_authentication_raises_unauthorised(
        self,
        beneficiary_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        new_beneficiary_payload,
    ):
        """Calling add_beneficiary with no owner_id must raise UnauthorisedError."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedError):
            beneficiary_service.add_beneficiary(
                owner_id=None,
                **new_beneficiary_payload,
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 5. NEGATIVE PATH — Insufficient Funds
# ===========================================================================


class TestInsufficientFunds:
    def test_transfer_exceeds_available_balance_raises_error(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """Transfer amount greater than balance must raise InsufficientFundsError."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer  # balance=5000
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=ACCOUNT_BALANCE + 0.01,  # one penny over balance
                reference="Overspend",
            )

    def test_transfer_equal_to_balance_succeeds(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        mock_transfer_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """Transfer equal to exact balance must succeed (boundary value)."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary
        mock_transfer_repo.save.side_effect = lambda t: t

        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
            owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
            beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
            amount=ACCOUNT_BALANCE,  # exactly 5000
            reference="Clear account",
        )

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.PENDING

    def test_transfer_of_zero_amount_raises_error(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """A zero-value transfer must raise InsufficientFundsError or ValueError."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises((InsufficientFundsError, ValueError)):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=0.00,
                reference="Nothing",
            )

    def test_transfer_of_negative_amount_raises_error(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """A negative transfer amount must raise ValueError."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=-50.00,
                reference="Reverse",
            )

    def test_insufficient_funds_transfer_not_persisted(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        mock_transfer_repo,
        authorised_customer,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """No transfer record must be created when funds are insufficient."""
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = authorised_customer
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=authorised_customer.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=ACCOUNT_BALANCE + 1,
                reference="Overspend",
            )

        mock_transfer_repo.save.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 6. NEGATIVE PATH — Exceeded Transfer Limits
# ===========================================================================


class TestExceededLimits:
    def test_single_transfer_exceeds_daily_limit_raises_error(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """A single transfer above the daily limit must raise TransferLimitExceededError."""
        wealthy_account = Account(
            account_id=str(uuid4()),
            owner_id="wealthy-001",
            balance=500_000.00,
            daily_transfer_limit=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT,  # 10,000
            daily_transferred=0.00,
            is_active=True,
        )
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = wealthy_account
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(TransferLimitExceededError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=wealthy_account.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT + 0.01,
                reference="Limit breach",
            )

    def test_cumulative_transfers_exceed_daily_limit_raises_error(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """Cumulative daily transfers exceeding the limit must be rejected."""
        account_near_limit = Account(
            account_id=str(uuid4()),
            owner_id="customer-near-limit",
            balance=500_000.00,
            daily_transfer_limit=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT,  # 10,000
            daily_transferred=9_500.00,              # already 9,500 sent
            is_active=True,
        )
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = account_near_limit
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(TransferLimitExceededError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=account_near_limit.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=600.00,  # 9500 + 600 = 10,100 > 10,000
                reference="Pushes over limit",
            )

    def test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_boundary_succeeds(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        mock_transfer_repo,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """Transfer that brings total exactly to the daily limit must succeed."""
        account_near_limit = Account(
            account_id=str(uuid4()),
            owner_id="customer-near-limit",
            balance=500_000.00,
            daily_transfer_limit=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT,  # 10,000
            daily_transferred=9_500.00,
            is_active=True,
        )
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = account_near_limit
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary
        mock_transfer_repo.save.side_effect = lambda t: t

        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
            owner_id=account_near_limit.owner_id,
            beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
            amount=500.00,  # 9500 + 500 = 10,000 exactly
            reference="Exact limit",
        )

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.PENDING

    def test_exceeded_limit_transfer_not_persisted(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        mock_transfer_repo,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """No transfer record must be saved when the daily limit is exceeded."""
        wealthy_account = Account(
            account_id=str(uuid4()),
            owner_id="wealthy-002",
            balance=500_000.00,
            daily_transfer_limit=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT,
            daily_transferred=0.00,
            is_active=True,
        )
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = wealthy_account
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(TransferLimitExceededError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=wealthy_account.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT + 1,
                reference="Over limit",
            )

        mock_transfer_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_limit_error_contains_remaining_allowance(
        self,
        transfer_service,
        mock_account_repo,
        mock_beneficiary_repo,
        active_beneficiary,
    ):
        """TransferLimitExceededError must expose the remaining daily allowance."""
        account_near_limit = Account(
            account_id=str(uuid4()),
            owner_id="customer-near-limit-2",
            balance=500_000.00,
            daily_transfer_limit=DEFAULT_DAILY_LIMIT,
            daily_transferred=9_500.00,
            is_active=True,
        )
        mock_account_repo.get_by_owner.return_value = account_near_limit
        mock_beneficiary_repo.get_by_id.return_value = active_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(TransferLimitExceededError) as exc_info:
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                owner_id=account_near_limit.owner_id,
                beneficiary_id=active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id,
                amount=600.00,
                reference="Pushes over limit",
            )

        assert exc_info.value.remaining_allowance == pytest.approx(500.00)
